repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 has scalar one-window endpoint programs, not a nested-window fixed-profile LP

published_prior_art.cite: RosenmanRajkumarGauriotSlonim2019: discrete manipulated-RD bounds via sequential convex programming, not a nested-window transport LP
GerardRokkanenRothe2020: sharp manipulated-RD bounds, but no many-window TU/min-cost-flow certificate found
GerardRokkanenRothe2020 and HahnToddVanDerKlaauw2001: one-window sharp-RD/manipulated-RD benchmark

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The constructive object is the expanded TU min-cost-flow network, but §9 only computes a fixed-m profile transport LP and does not instantiate the outer mass-profile network from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §4 results-at-a-glance consumer is generic: 'auditable computation of manipulation-robust pooled RD endpoints' names no paper, workflow, software target, or applied domain.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation rubric only partially delivered: the draft adds a fixed-profile LP/exhibit, but the LP breaks common retained-plan compatibility and the promised outer TU flow object is not exhibited.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The transport reduction is algebraically false for a common retained shell plan: q_jw(m)=m_j pi_jw-m_{j-1} pi_{j-1,w} is the retained mass increment from shell j, but the LP allows earlier-shell atoms b<j to satisfy later increment demands, so m_j no longer equals cumulative retained shell mass.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The expanded min-cost-flow object is underdefined: the objective coefficients c_j(m)=omega_j/m_j depend nonlinearly on endogenous retained masses, but no linearization or finite network construction is specified.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: §10 hand-waves the key step 'represent mass-profile constraints by chain arcs' and gives no concrete algebraic route from the variable-denominator outer problem to a node-arc-incidence min-cost-flow formulation.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the invalid b<=j transport arcs with shell-compatible increment constraints, then recompute whether any nontrivial flow structure remains after each q_jw is tied to shell j.

If the flagship target is still a computation result, give a §9 exhibit for the full outer retained-mass optimization, including all network nodes, arcs, costs, balances, and a dual certificate from observable primitives.
State a concrete downstream consumer for Conjecture 1, such as a named density-testing/RD software workflow or a many-window empirical RD reporting practice tied to a cited paper.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; the drafter needs a fresh computation kernel because the fixed-profile transport identity does not represent the stated common retained-plan model.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal attempts the computation upgrade, but the fixed-profile transport formulation is not sound and the outer TU flow certificate remains promissory.
